Name: The College Dropout
Notes: This cover art features the dropout bear on the cover of Kanye's second studio album, Late Registration, and later became an animated character for Kanye's Graduation album cover. The music video for the opening track "Good Morning" features this same bear becoming a cartoon character. Funnily enough, this bear wasn't planned to be on the cover; it just happened to be at the high school where the photoshoot took place.

Name: Late Registration
Notes: The cover for Late Registration was designed by Brooklyn graphic design studio Morning Breath, Inc. It shows Kanye's mascot, the Dropout Bear, standing between entrance doors to a college, continuing the educational themes of Kanye's previous effort The College Dropout. The cover was inspired by the works of satirical American painter John Currin, one of Kanye's favorite artists.
Designer: Louis Marino, Kris Yiengst & Morning Breath, Inc.

Name: Late Registration [Booklet]
Notes: From designer Louis Marino, "We went through many concepts to communicate this from having a young Kanye create the bear out of legos, to developing an entire class of fully articulated 4' robotic puppets. After many rounds of creative (and budget) meetings, it was decided to stick with a 4' robotic bear and to have him move about a university campus always late. The location was to resemble a "Harry Potter" quality, so we shot on location at Princeton University. I was able to get Louis Vuitton designed a custom backpack for the bear."

Name: Graduation
Notes: Takashi Murakami says about the work: "The cover is based on Kanye's theme of student life. School. It's a place of dreams, of righteousness, a place to have fun. It's also occasionally a place where you experience the rigid dogma of the human race. Kanye's music scrapes sentimentality and aggressiveness together like sandpaper, and he uses his grooves to unleash this tornado that spins with the zeitgeist of the times. I too wanted to be swept up and spun around in that tornado."

Name: Impossible / Drive Slow
Notes: This song was recorded for the 2006 film Mission Impossible 3. It was played during the ending credits, however, it was not part of the official soundtrack. An explicit version of the track has never been released to the public, and only clean versions are available to stream/purchase. It supposedly took 50 hours to make. While "Impossible" was left on the cutting-room floor for Twista's album, West found it to be the perfect offering when Tom Cruise called.

Name: Stronger
Notes: The is the single art for the July 31st, 2007 release of "Stronger". The art was done by Japanese artist Takashi Murakami. Mukakami also designed the cover art for the album and the later KIDS SEE GHOSTS. The two heads on the bottom represent Daft Punk, whose "Harder Better Faster Stronger" was sampled as the instrumental.

Name: Good Life

The variety of colors and fonts of these words is also reference to the song's music video which features large letters and a variety of colors coming out of Kanye's mouth as he raps the song.

Name: 808s & Heartbreak [Cover 1]
Notes: Straying from the colorful, striking artworks from Ye's previous albums, the artwork for 808s & Heartbreak, consists of an almost featureless background, saving room for a deflated heart-shaped balloon and a small color palette to the left, in which each color represents a song from the album, with one exception. It's about the last song "Pinocchio Story", which signifies this song as the one where he truly poured out his heart (hence, the deflated heart in the center).
Designer: Past Tell Museum, JP Robinson, Kristen Yiengst & Virgil Abloh

Name: 808s & Heartbreak [Cover 2]
Notes: This version of the 808s & Heartbreak album art is from the visual artist/graphic designer Kaws. The distinctive hands with the Xs on them display his personal and unique art in this cover. The hands are splitting the heart into two representing a heartbreak. Kanye's losses the past years reflect this and the whole album itself.

Name: 808s & Heartbreak [Rough Draft Cover]
Notes: 808s & Heartbreak cover leaked by waterfalls as a bonus for the "Never Forgive Yourself" groupbuy. Has a watermark. Reflects the style of the singles, but was likely made prior to KAWS coming on to design art for the album. Some of the single art was made without KAWS' involvement, implying art direction was done before he was involved which this may be from.

Name: 808s & Heartbreak [Booklet]
Notes: Full 808s & Heartbreak booklet featuring art from KAWs, album credits, and a picture of Kanye and Donda West, whose passing heavily influenced the creation and themes of the album.

Name: Heartless

Name: Amazing [Alternate Cover]

Name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy
Notes: West came to Condo's studio, where for several hours they listened to tapes of his music, and over the next few days Condo made eight or nine paintings and a lurid scene of a naked black man on a bed, straddled by a naked white female creature with fearsome features, wings, no arms, and a long, spotted tail. West chose that one. Ye insisted on a cover that would be so outlandish it would be outlawed. He got his wish. The album cover ended up being blurred.
Designer: Kanye West, Don C., Plain Pat, Virgil Abloh, George Condo, M/M, JP Robinson, Todd Russell & Kristen Yiengst

Name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy [Alternate Cover 1]
Notes: Uncensored version of the streaming cover. Meant to be the main album cover until the art was forced to be censored.
Designer: Kanye West, Don C., Plain Pat, Virgil Abloh, George Condo, M/M, JP Robinson, Todd Russell & Kristen Yiengst

Name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy [Alternate Cover 2]
Notes: This cover was used as the main cover on the CD and vinyl releases of the album. Ballerinas played a large part in this whole era, so it's fitting that one of the covers for the album featured one.
Designer: Kanye West, Don C., Plain Pat, Virgil Abloh, George Condo, M/M, JP Robinson, Todd Russell & Kristen Yiengst

Name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy [Alternate Cover 3]
Notes: The picture is actually the same one used in the cover for the "POWER" single. Kanye also used it as his Twitter profile picture for many years.
Designer: Kanye West, Don C., Plain Pat, Virgil Abloh, George Condo, M/M, JP Robinson, Todd Russell & Kristen Yiengst

Name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy [Alternate Cover 4]
Notes: One of many covers for this album that features Kanye with multiple mouths wide open, as was part of George Condo's signature style.
Designer: Kanye West, Don C., Plain Pat, Virgil Abloh, George Condo, M/M, JP Robinson, Todd Russell & Kristen Yiengst

Name: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy [Alternate Cover 5]

This is actually one of only three GOOD Friday singles without a parental advisory sticker on it.

The reason this cover doesn't mention Rick Ross is because he wasn't on the single version of the song, as he hadn't recorded for it yet.

Name: Watch The Throne
Notes: Designed by Givenchy creative director Riccardo Tisci, this masterpiece envelops a bold take on symmetrical, geometrical artwork. According to a Givenchy representative, "…angular lines were incorporated symbolically to convey the masculinity of two of the most iconic rap figures of our time." The same rep mentioned that the cover was designed to seem embroidered. The realistic light shading creates a 3-D effect that makes the image pop out.
Designer: Riccardo Tisci, Virgil Abloh & Joe Zeff

Name: Watch The Throne [Animated Cover]
Notes: Animated cover made for the streaming version of Watch The Throne.

Name: Watch The Throne [Alternate Cover]
Notes: Original art designed by Givenchy creative director Riccardo Tisci, before the golden embevelling effect was added. Matches the creative style of the "H•A•M" cover art and early "Why I Love You" covers much closer. Image attached is taken from the digital booklet because the original art without the creative director text hasn't been released, so this is technically a recreation of the original cover evern though it is the genuine art Riccardo Tisci created.

Name: Watch The Throne [Booklet]
Notes: Booklet made for the vinyl version of Watch The Throne.

Also features Cam'ron, Jim Jones, Vado, CyHi, Pusha T, Musiq Soulchild, Big Sean, and Teyana Taylor. Same as the streaming cover, but with more names.

Name: Cruel Summer
Notes: Joe Perez designed the cover for Cruel Summer. He posted a timelapse video of the creative process for the album cover above. Joe Perez has done many covers for Kanye West, including the covers for two of his studio albums; Yeezus and The Life Of Pablo. But Kanye isn't the only person he's done covers for; you can check his website to see all the covers he's done.

Name: Yeezus
Notes: This minimalist cover displays a CD with red tape on the side, appearing like many bargain bin disks would. One of Kanye's major influences going into the project was his newfound love of minimalist art. This minimalism is a stark contrast with his last album My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy, which had 5 different covers and the whole album being an artistic statement. This album had a minimalist approach to it and Kanye's influences and the cover represent that.

Name: Lil Wayne - I Am Not A Human Being II
Notes: Lil Wayne announced in November of 2012 that Kanye was designing the cover art and packaging for his album I Am Not A Human Being II. The cover officially debuted on January 30th, 2013. Lil Wayne explained the cover by saying, "he chose the moth butterfly thing because it has so many different stages of life, and it goes through so many forms and changes, and no one can figure it out, and it's always beautiful".

Name: The Life Of Pablo
Notes:
OG Filename: THE-LIFE-OF-PABLO-2-KANYE-PDP-layered
Kanye compared himself to artist Pablo Picasso, infamous drug lord Pablo Escobar and poet Pablo Neruda. With this in mind, people began wondering which of the three Pablos was Kanye referring to in the album title. The album artwork might represent the dichotomy between Kanye, the faithful family man (emphasis on faithful, as it covers both the denotation and faith in God), and Kanye being tempted by worldly pleasures as such as big booty women.
Designer: Joe Perez, Mark Seekings & Peter De Potter

Name: The Life Of Pablo [Alternate Cover]
Notes:
OG Filename: THE-LIFE-OF-PABLO-1-KANYE-PDP-layered 
Original cover art revealed for The Life Of Pablo. The image on the bottom left corner was taken at Kanye's parents' wedding. This photo is significant, as Ye has a deep feeling of affection and love for his family, which is a pivotal theme throughout the album. The chaotic text may have been inspired by The Shining. One scene in the film shows Wendy Torrance discovering the main antagonist's stack of obsessively typed pages, all repeating the same sentence: "All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy." Also used for the clean release.
Designer: Joe Perez, Mark Seekings & Peter De Potter

Notes: According to information in The WRLDFMS Tony Williams' emails, Kanye wanted a "offensive symbol" as the album cover. This is speculated to be a swastika.

Name: LOVE EVERYONE [Cover Idea]
Notes: Posted to Twitter with the text caption "This is my album cover" it was a picture of the surgeon deemed responsible for his mother's death. Thus the name LOVE EVERYONE implying even people like him. Nonetheless, the doctor requested not to have his likeness on the cover, thus forcing a new cover to be made. We don't have the actual official cover, but we have a very good idea of what the cover would look like based on the image Kanye sent.

Name: le [Asset?]
Notes: Not the album cover for LE, simply an image found in a zip file for an early version of the album, unknown what it could have been used for, but might've been meant for some form of promotional artwork. The filename is le. The name of the album was not LOVE EVERYONE at the time, so "le" does not refer to LOVE EVERYONE but something else like "Long Exposure".

Name: DAYTONA
Notes: Official cover art for DAYTONA. The cover is a slightly-edited version of a photograph of Whitney Houston's bathroom. Pusha T connected the cover art to his album, saying that the photo represents "organized chaos" and "sonically that's what my album feels like."

Name: ye
Notes: The cover art features the mountains of Jackson Hole, Wyoming on the night of Ye's album listening party. The green lettering captures the last-minute character of the cover art, looking as if it has been scribbled on the photo at short notice. The caption appears to be a paradox, but it is basically a bipolar statement about being bipolar, emphasizing Ye's mental state: He hates being bipolar (probably when in his depression phases), but then he loves it (probably when in his euphoria phases).

Name: ye [No Text]
Notes: Original photo taken on an iPhone 8+ with no text found on a website used to promote the release of ye. Most likely what happened is that Timothy Bauman (designer of the website) asked Kanye for the raw photo with no text, and Kanye gave him the OG file.

Name: ye [Rough Draft Cover 1]
Notes: Original cover of ye posted by engineer yesjulz the day of the listening party. On the last day of the album, according to Francis and the Lights in a Reddit AMA, "Mr. West talked about how he is like a tree... in that, like some of the legendary trees, the world simply won't allow it to be chopped down. also in that, by living, he is providing humanity with some essential ingredient. All the while, he absorbs the negative output of humanity. Also fun and you can climb all over it if you're a little kid."

Name: ye [Rough Draft Cover 2]
Notes: Features an image of Brittany Murphy, an American actress who passed away in 2009 under mysterious circumstances after taking perscription drugs to ease her pneumonia and anemia. Not an actual square.

Name: ye [Rough Draft Cover 3]
Notes: Similar to the previous ye cover, but with a different background and the text being in a different space. Still not an actual square. Uses a Bob Ross painting titled "misty waterfall".

Notes: Removing the image of Brittany Murphy, making the text bigger and green, and adding a new background, this one is an actual square. Uses a Bob Ross painting titled "misty waterfall".

Name: ye [Rough Draft Cover 6]
Notes: Same as the previous cover, but the text is red and it's more rectangular. Not a square sadly. Uses a Bob Ross painting titled "misty waterfall".

Name: ye [Rough Draft Cover 7]

Name: KIDS SEE GHOSTS
Notes: The image is based on Katsushika Hokusai's swastika Fuji. Visual elements observed in both artworks include the backdrop of Mount Fuji, Murakami's Oval Characters that are painted atop the other as well as a pair of bending trees. The positioning of these elements in each piece remains the same except for the Oval characters in Kids being minimized and placed more to the left. The most fascinating component is the inverted Kanji characters in Kids that translates to "Chaos."

Name: KIDS SEE GHOSTS [Rough Draft Cover]
Notes: Art done by Takashi Murakami for KIDS SEE GHOSTS on a whiteboard. Posted by Ye on April 21, 2018 showing rough draft of the artwork.

Name: NASIR
Notes: Official cover art for NASIR. The cover is a photo taken by Mary Ellen Mark for a news feature in the November 1988 issue of the Texas Monthly magazine. The feature was titled "The War Zone" and was a field report of a Dallas neighborhood that was dealing with the crack epidemic. The photo was also used for one of Playboi Carti's first mixtapes, titled Killing Me Softly.

Name: Yandhi

This cover is a photoshopped version of a picture from the Japanese Wikipedia article for Minidiscs, replacing the grey band on the side with purple and brightening it up. Special shoutout to Kuha455405 for being the one to put the original image on Wikipedia in 2009.

Name: JESUS IS KING
Notes: The official artwork for JESUS IS KING is an image of the album's vinyl disc. It features the bright blue hue that has been connected to the album throughout its promotion. This cover has an indirect correlation to some of Ye's previous work, particularly Yeezus. "AR 1331 A" is apparently the "Archer Records (AR) pressing plant code" assigned to a 1970 Detroit gospel single. However, unlike the original record, the JIK vinyl changed "45 RPM EP" to "33 RPM LP."

Blue is a common color seen in Christianity that is used to symbolize sky, representing Heaven, eternity, and truth in religion. The water seen on the art's cover is a common symbol in the religion, as Holy Water is a symbol of purity and cleansing of sin within the faith.

Name: Donda
Notes: The cover art for Donda is a blank, almost black space. Amidst the rollout of the album, the color black had made frequent appearances. During the second listening party for the album, which happened on August 5, 2021, at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta, hundreds of performers had all dressed in nothing, but black, including Kanye himself. Reportedly, the staff of the stadium, chefs, as well as all engineers were required to wear black as well for a period of time. Donning dark colors for mourning has been strongly associated with death and loss for centuries in the west and is a practice believed to date back to the Roman times. Not actually black, but #020305.

Name: Donda [Alternate Cover 1]
Notes: This album cover is inspired by an art piece by French-American artist Louise Bourgeois. The original artwork was part of a collection titled Les têtes bleues et les femmes rouges, which translates as The blue heads and the red women. Bourgeois would often make art to work through the trauma of losing her mother at a young age. This theme resonates with Kanye's dedication of DONDA to his late mother, who passed away in 2007.

Name: Donda [Alternate Cover 2]

Name: VULTURES 1
Notes: The album cover for VULTURES 1 features Kanye West on the left and his wife Bianca Censori on the right. The couple presents itself, poised within a space adorned in subtle hues of gray. Kanye is entirely clad in black attire, including a black hockey mask, as he faces the viewer. Bianca Censori, partially nude, turns away from the observer, attired in long black stockings, black shoes and a very thin, transparent cloth around her waist. Kanye West & Ty Dolla $ign's collaborative album was released on February 10, 2024; the 20th anniversary of the release of his debut album The College Dropout. This is the only cover in Ye's discography featuring Ye's actual body besides The College Dropout. The cover art was photographed by visual director Aus Taylor, who was at first surprised by its usage over the Burzum-like one: "ye dawg thank you so much for trusting me blindly"

Name: VULTURES 2
Notes: The official cover art for VULTURES 2. It features the same styling as the cover for VULTURES 1, but replaces Kanye West and his wife Bianca Censori in favor of Ty Dolla $ign. Ty holds to his chest a photo of his older brother Big TC, who got incarcerated in 2004 to spend the rest of his life in prison for murder. TC alleges to be innocent of the killing. The image in the frame was uploaded to Big TC's Instagram in 2019. Ty has shown a lot of support for his brother's freedom campaign in the past, titling his debut studio album after it and called for TC's freedom on the titular track of VULTURES 1.

Name: VULTURES 2 [Concept Art]
Notes: According to the Big Boy interview, Kanye was in Paris with Pharrell, Aus, and others. During that time, a sketch was drawn of Ty holding a photo of TC, which everybody liked. He said at one point that they should release the sketch, but they have not as of yet.

Notes: Cover for the "WW3" single. Features a black KKK hood on a black background. The original, unedited version of the cover was posted by Ye to Twitter on March 18th, 2025.

Name: WW3
Notes: Posted by The Game on April 3rd 2025. Photo of a KKK wedding from 2015. Edited to remove a dog that was present in the original photo, and also change the race of the white robe KKK member from white to black. Uses AI to fill in the background with more hay bales.

Name: COUSINS
Notes: Cover for the "COUSINS" single. Is the painting "Leda mit dem Schwan" by Paul Mathias Padua, which depicts the Greek myth "Leda and the Swan", in which the Greek god Zeus morphs into a Swan and seduces the Spartan queen Leda. Has historically been claimed to have been one of Hitlers favorite paintings.

Name: COUSINS [Alternate Cover 1]
Notes: Alternate cover for "COUSINS" posted by Ye on Twitter. Is "Mother and Child" by Viktor Koretsky, an anti-Nazi propaganda piece created during WWII.

Name: COUSINS [Alternate Cover 2]
Notes: Alternate cover for "COUSINS" posted by Ye on Twitter. Comes from the "Kloran", the KKK handbook - used to detail the ceremonies and procedures of the KKK.

Name: COUSINS [Alternate Cover 3]
Notes: Alternate cover for "COUSINS" posted by Ye on Twitter. Depicts William Jospeh Simmons, the leader/founder of the second interation of the KKK.
